ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-02-2025 Continued To Feb-19-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-26-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT FLORAFLEX CORP.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Defendant Floraflex Corp.'s demurrer to the sole cause of action in the complaint filed by plaintiff Keep America Safe and Beautiful (KASB) is overruled.

The current demurrer is not untimely. KASB misinterprets the register of actions entry of September 3, 2024. That entry took the prior demurrer off calendar for failure to provide courtesy copies and authorized the motion to be re-set after September 19, 2024. The current demurrer complies with that entry.

Floraflex's contention that pre-notice is deficient lacks merit. Section VI of the notice directed communications to KASB's counsel, Rebecca M. Jackson, and provided her address and phone number. Floraflex does not explain why KASB's counsel is not a "responsible individual" within the meaning of the Proposition 65 regulations, nor is the court aware of any authority or reason why counsel may not qualify as a responsible individual. Accordingly, Ms. Jackson is a "responsible individual" CCR 25903(b)(2)(A)(1).

Floraflex's argument that KASB did not attach a copy of the required summary of Proposition 65 with its notice also fails. The absence of a summary on the Office of the Attorney General's website does not show that KASB failed to append the summary when it mailed notice to Floraflex. Other attachments listed in the notice are also omitted from the website. The proof of service states that the summary was served by mail to Floraflex's CEO. Absent directly controverting evidence, the presumption of Evidence Code 641 governs.

Order is signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/HEK)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-06-2024 Continued To Sep-17-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar May-10-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. BRIAN JOHNSON, Esq. of Seven Hills LLP (tel: 415-926-7247) for Plaintiff.
Atty. KEVIN COLE, Esq. of KJC Law Group, APC (tel: 310-861-7797) for Defendant.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT FLORAFLEX CORP.'s MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion to vacate the default entered on March 15, 2023, is granted.

Defendant seeks relief based on equity/extrinsic mistake. Defendant must show a meritorious case, satisfactory excuse for not presenting a defense, and diligence. (Rappleyea v. Campbell (1994) 8 Cal.4th 975, 982.) Defendant meets its burden.

"One ground for equitable relief is extrinsic mistake-a term broadly applied when circumstances extrinsic to the litigation have unfairly cost a party a hearing on the merits." (Rappleyea v. Campbell (1994) 8 Cal.4th 975, 981.) Here, defendant failed to respond because it was unfamiliar with the litigation process and became confused by the notice of violation and additional letters from plaintiff. (Martin Decl., pars. 2-7.) Defendant states a meritorious defense, e.g., plaintiff tested a competitor's product. Lastly, defendant demonstrates diligence. After learning of the default, the parties engaged in settlement discussions, which delayed the filing of this motion. (Guizar Decl., par. 4; Cole Decl., pars. 3-4.)

The court notes that plaintiff's opposition is essentially non-responsive since it focuses on the requirements of CCP 473, and defendant bought this motion on the independent ground of equity.

The court exercises its equitable powers to vacate the dismissal of the Doe defendants entered on April 21, 2023, since that dismissal was filed solely to perfect the default judgment and the court is vacating the default.

Defendant to file response to the complaint by May 31, 2024.

Order signed.

Judge: Rochelle C. East; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RCE)
